title: Transmission Control Protocol
description: Reliable, ordered, connection-oriented transport protocol guaranteeing delivery.
fullExplanation: TCP provides reliable, ordered, error-checked delivery of a stream of bytes between applications. It establishes a connection via 3-way handshake before data transfer. Sequence numbers track every byte. Acknowledgements confirm receipt. The sliding window controls how much data can be in-flight. Congestion control algorithms (CUBIC, BBR) prevent network overload. TCP is used by HTTP, HTTPS, SSH, SMTP, FTP — any application needing guaranteed delivery.

fields.name: Sequence Number
fields.description: Position of first byte in this segment

fields.name: Ack Number
fields.description: Next expected byte from other side

fields.name: Flags
fields.description: URG ACK PSH RST SYN FIN control bits

fields.name: Urgent Pointer
fields.description: Points to urgent data if URG flag set

keyFacts: Window scaling option allows windows up to 1GB
CUBIC is default congestion control in Linux
BBR (Google) models bandwidth instead of reacting to loss
SYN cookies prevent SYN flood without storing state
TCP Fast Open reduces latency for repeat connections
